---
title: "R Markdown + Quarto for Reproducible Science"
subtitle: "A One-Day Workshop for NIEHS Scientists"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

## About this workshop

This is a hands-on introduction to **R Markdown** and **Quarto**: tools for combining narrative, code, tables, figures, and citations in a reproducible report.

R Markdown remains supported and widely used. Quarto is a newer, multi-language publishing system with additional built-in features. You do not need to abandon existing `.Rmd` files; the bridge module shows how the same mental model carries into `.qmd` files. See the [official Quarto FAQ for R Markdown users](https://quarto.org/docs/faq/rmarkdown.html).

::: {.callout-important}
## The outcome that matters
By 5 PM, you will be able to render a Quarto HTML report from a clean session containing narrative text, an inline result, a table, a figure, a caption, a cross-reference, and a citation—and explain how an `.Rmd` maps to a `.qmd`.
:::

## How you will work today

The training website is **read-only course material**. Your editable files live in `student_work/`, separate from the website:

```text
student_work/morning_report.Rmd
student_work/my_report.qmd
```

Use the `.Rmd` file for the two short morning lessons. After the bridge, keep `my_report.qmd` open and add one useful element at each stage. Because `student_work/` has its own `_quarto.yml`, rendering your report will not trigger the course website or unrelated lesson files.

Checkpoint files are available in `student_work/checkpoints/` if you need to recover from an editing problem.

## Who this is for

- NIEHS scientists, postdocs, and trainees who want a reproducible analysis-and-writing workflow.
- Participants comfortable reading basic R code.
- No prior R Markdown or Quarto experience is required.
- Python is optional and is demonstrated rather than required.

## Learning priorities

| Level | Content |
|---|---|
| **Everyone does** | Clean-session render, YAML, chunks, inline values, one `kable()` table, one `ggplot2` figure, captions, figure alt text, cross-references, and one citation |
| **Instructor demonstrates** | Revealjs, parameters, DOCX output, project configuration, and safe sharing |
| **Bonus/reference** | Python, `DT`, `plotly`, `patchwork`, `broom`, PDF/LaTeX, deep theming, batch rendering, public publishing, and Quartobot |

## Schedule

| Time | Focus | Delivery |
|---|---|---|
| 09:00–09:15 | Setup and student workspace | Everyone renders `student_work/my_report.qmd` |
| 09:15–10:00 | R Markdown anatomy, clean render, chunks, inline code | One cumulative exercise |
| 10:00–10:15 | Break | |
| 10:15–10:35 | R Markdown → Quarto bridge | Convert one small file |
| 10:35–11:10 | First Quarto report | YAML, callout, code folding |
| 11:10–12:10 | Code options and tables | Everyone builds one `kable()` table; advanced methods are demonstrations |
| 12:10–13:10 | Lunch | |
| 13:10–13:55 | Figures | One ggplot, caption, alt text, and label |
| 13:55–14:40 | Cross-references and citations | Equations are a brief demonstration |
| 14:40–14:55 | Break | |
| 14:55–15:20 | Revealjs | Instructor demo plus one learner edit |
| 15:20–15:45 | Parameters | Instructor demo plus one parameter change |
| 15:45–16:20 | Cumulative capstone | Polish the report already built |
| 16:20–16:40 | Projects and safe sharing | No live theme-editing exercise |
| 16:40–17:00 | Debrief, troubleshooting, exit ticket | Save/export work and state one next action |

Total contact time is **6 hours 30 minutes**, plus two 15-minute breaks and a 60-minute lunch.

## Data used in examples

The workshop uses `data/workshop_biomarkers.csv`, a **synthetic teaching dataset** with 200 simulated records. It contains no participant data. Relationships were intentionally generated so figures and models are visible during class; they are not evidence about PFAS exposure or health outcomes.

## Before we begin

You need a browser and access to the instructor-provided Posit Cloud project. Account creation and login should be completed before class whenever possible.

Open [`00_setup.qmd`](00_setup.qmd) when the instructor signals.

## Materials and licensing

Training materials are released under CC BY 4.0 unless otherwise noted. Reuse with attribution.
